candidate_problems.text: What precise organizational criteria are necessary and sufficient for subjective experience, rather than merely sophisticated control? The conjecture points to self-modeling, global availability, recursive feedback, and causal indispensability, but it leaves unresolved where the threshold lies between thin regulation (like a thermostat), complex unconscious control, and genuine subjectivity.

Why should this interaction structure have an intrinsic point of view at all, rather than only producing functionally integrated behavior? The central explanatory gap remains: even if recursive self-modeling explains unified self-regulation, it is still open whether and how such organization entails 'what it is like' instead of only observer-described information processing.

How can the conjecture be operationalized into testable measures that distinguish degrees and kinds of experience across different systems? If consciousness depends on organizational variables such as integration, temporal depth, bandwidth of self-modeling, and internally enacted boundaries, a major open problem is defining these rigorously enough to compare brains, animals, AI systems, and edge cases without circularity.
